1.1: Maret Coll — the hall-keeper; word on the road is she pays for trouble taken off the drove-roads, and Cadell means to find her first.

2.1: The drove-roads — his own country, the long lowland ways he walked up to get here; he reads a road the way others read a face.
2.2: The stock-pens — talk on the road said this settlement's beasts are vanishing; the kind of trouble that has coin behind it.

content._: What cadell has come to know — names recognised; absence means perceived by appearance.
